era_context: Romantic piano culture centered on Paris salons, teaching, and publishing; Polish origin and diasporic identity are recurrent themes in reception and in his own letters and works.
public_impression: Often remembered as the archetypal composer of intimate piano lyricism: mazurkas, polonaises, nocturnes, and études associated with refinement, singing line, and national color.
deeper_dimensions: His artistic world also includes large-scale forms (ballades, scherzos, sonatas), formal experimentation within dance genres, and a documented engagement with pedagogy, orchestration in early works, and the economics of publishing and performance networks.

personality.notes: Letter evidence supports care for reputation and health struggles; temperament summaries in popular accounts often flatten nuance, so this profile stays descriptive rather than diagnostic.

aims: sustain vocal bel canto analogies in piano texture
embed folkloric gesture without reducing works to postcard nationalism
artistic_identity.notes: Aims are phrased as compositional tendencies visible in analysis and reception, not as self-maxims transcribed from missing manifestos.

characteristic_elements: rubato treated as structural rather than merely ornamental in many works
ornamental variation across repeats and editions
dance genres as carriers of large-scale drama as well as charm
musical_style.notes: Stylistic bullets summarize widely taught features; they are not exhaustive and elide manuscript-editorial debates best handled outside this file.

habits: revision visible across parallel versions and editions
workshop-like refinement in teaching contexts feeding public performance repertory
creative_process.notes: Process claims lean on edition scholarship and pedagogy traces; daily routines are not fully recoverable from sources.

source_notes: Life dates and Paris-centric career framing are standard reference points. Personality and process lines blend documentary hints (letters, teaching lineage) with interpretive synthesis common in musicology and performance traditions; they are modeling tools for creative simulation, not clinical or biographical verdicts.
